page: Amazon Web Services m8gn.16xlarge is a general-purpose instance powered by AWS Graviton ARMv9 Neoverse-V2 processors. It features 64 dedicated physical cores operating at 2.8 GHz with a 1.0 thread-per-core topology. The memory configuration consists of 256.0 GB of RAM, providing a balanced 4.0 GB per core ratio. This instance does not include local NVMe storage or GPU accelerators, but it delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 200 Gbps under the Nitro hypervisor.

Benchmark data indicates top-tier memory performance, with memory bandwidth read and write operations consistently placing in the top 10% of tested systems. Multi-core capabilities are strong, as demonstrated by top-tier results in multi-threaded bzip3 decompression, Geekbench multi-core scores, and Passmark CPU physics tests. However, single-core performance is more moderate, with stress-ng single-core and Passmark CPU encryption tests scoring in the middle 50%. In database and web serving scenarios, the instance achieves top-tier results, notably in Redis SET operations and static web serving throughput.

While the instance excels in multi-threaded processing and memory throughput, it exhibits a notable performance tradeoff in low-parameter language model text generation, where text generation for a small 135M model falls into the bottom 10%. Conversely, text generation for medium (7B) and large (70B) models ranks in the top 25%. Given the high network bandwidth, balanced memory-to-core ratio, and strong multi-core efficiency, this instance is suited for database operations, software compilation, static web serving, and multi-threaded data processing workloads.
description: Amazon Web Services m8gn.16xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 64 dedicated ARMv9 Neoverse-V2 physical cores running at 2.8 GHz, paired with 256.0 GB of RAM. Operating on the Nitro hypervisor, it provides 200 Gbps of baseline network bandwidth but lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. Benchmark results show top-tier memory bandwidth and multi-core performance, particularly in database operations, static web serving, and software compilation. Single-core performance and text generation for small language models represent key tradeoffs, scoring in the average and poor ranges respectively. However, multi-threaded tasks and larger model text generation perform strongly. This hardware profile is suited for network-optimized database workloads, web serving, and multi-threaded data processing. Qualitatively, the ARM-based architecture offers high resource density and memory throughput relative to its compute footprint, making it a cost-efficient option for scale-out multi-threaded applications.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m8gn.16xlarge is an ARM64 general-purpose instance with 64 physical cores, 256 GB RAM, and 200 Gbps network bandwidth, delivering top-tier memory bandwidth and multi-core performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m8gn.16xlarge is an ARM64 general-purpose instance featuring 64 physical cores, 256 GB RAM, and 200 Gbps baseline network bandwidth.
tagline: An ARM64 general-purpose instance featuring sixty-four dedicated cores, high-speed memory bandwidth, and baseline network throughput of two hundred gigabits per second.
bullet_points: 64 dedicated ARMv9 Neoverse-V2 physical cores at 2.8 GHz
256.0 GB of RAM with top-tier memory bandwidth performance
200 Gbps baseline network bandwidth under Nitro hypervisor
No local NVMe storage or GPU accelerators included
Suited for database operations, web serving, and software compilation
